_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Thoracoabdominal aneurysms (TAAs) develop in patients with atherosclerotic disease, chronic aortic dissection, and connective tissue disorders (e.g., Marfan syndrome, Ehler-Danlos syndrome)
Risk of rupture increases rapidly when aneurysmal diameter reaches or exceeds 6 cm
Presentation includes abdominal or back pain

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication of graft requirement, and planned line of management

claim.label: Clinical Evaluation of the brain function during the procedure (Intra-operative monitoring documentation)
Graft details - barcode/invoice (if artificial graft used)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes all vitals, symptoms, signs, physical examination, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment.
Did the clinical condition and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Does the severity and imaging indicate surgery?
Was graft detail (barcode/invoice) available in case of artificial graft?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical condition, severity, or imaging was not indicative of surgery.
Diagnosis not confirmed by clinical condition and imaging.
Artificial graft details (barcode/invoice) missing or incomplete at claim stage.
CT/MRI/Angiography not provided or insufficient for diagnosis/surgical planning.
Documentation of clinical evaluation of brain function during the procedure is missing or incomplete.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at a Tertiary Hospital with equipped facilities for bypass
clinical_key_pointers: Thoracoabdominal aneurysms (TAAs) develop in patients with atherosclerotic disease, chronic aortic dissection, and connective tissue disorders (e.g., Marfan syndrome, Ehler-Danlos syndrome).
Pathologic processes include chronic inflammation, remodeling of extracellular matrix, and depletion of vascular smooth muscle cells.
Elastin depletion in the media layer of the aortic wall is a common finding.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Medical therapy includes the reduction of risk factors for aneurysmal expansion (e.g., smoking, hypercholesterolemia) and tight blood pressure control predicated on the use of beta-blockers.
